authorization.mibModalText.BCL.pqNWvp:
You can think of it like a credit report for the {productIsLife, select, true{life insurance} other{insurance}} process. Whenever you submit {productIsLife, select, true{a life} other{an}} insurance application to any insurance company, those records get sent to MIB. There’s no penalty or cost to you for us to check your records. {br} {br} It’s a resource we’ll use when we’re processing your application. It allows us to check past records to uncover whether there may have been errors, omissions or misrepresentations made on any of your previous {productIsLife, select, true{life insurance} other{insurance}} applications. {br} {br}
MIB Pre-Notice
{br} Information regarding your insurability will be treated as confidential. Blue Cross Life Insurance Company of Canada or its reinsurers may, however, make a brief report thereon to MIB, LLC, which operates an information exchange on behalf of insurance companies that are members of MIB Group Inc. If you apply to another MIB Member company for life or health insurance coverage, or a claim for benefits is submitted to such a company, MIB, upon request, will supply such company with the information in its file. {br} {br} Upon receipt of a request from you MIB will arrange disclosure of any information it may have in your file. Please contact MIB at *866-692-6901 or go to its website at www.mib.com to request disclosure online. If you question the accuracy of information in MIB’s file, you may contact MIB and seek a correction in accordance with the procedures set forth in the federal Fair Credit Reporting Act. The address of MIB’s information office is 50 Braintree Hill Park, Suite 400 Braintree, MA 02184- 8734. {br} {br} Blue Cross Life Insurance Company of Canada, or its reinsurers, may also release information in its file to other insurance companies to whom you may apply for life or health insurance, or to whom a claim for benefits may be submitted. Information for consumers about MIB may be obtained on its website at www.mib.com.{br} {br}

backedByBillionsModal.ciModal.xWTflX:
In Canada, critical illness insurance is highly regulated in order to protect you. This means that your policy is covered by four layers of protection, including:
  1. PolicyMe (hi, that’s us!)
  2. Our insurance partner, Canadian Premier
  3. Our reinsurance partner
  4. Assuris, a non-profit that protects all Canadian policyholders.
In the highly unlikely event that any critical illness insurance company no longer existed, Assuris would cover up to 85% of a customer’s policy or $60,000 in coverage, whichever is higher.
So, breathe easy. You and your loved ones are protected.

ciAddonCard.tooltipText.lyZpi0:
Critical illness insurance protects you from unexpected medical and change-in-lifestyle expenses that are not covered by provincial health care insurance.

That means if ever you’re diagnosed with one of the illnesses covered by this policy, you would receive a lump-sum payment in the amount of the coverage amount you select.

It would help you cover any expenses while you’re temporarily off work so that you can focus on what matters most: your recovery.

ciCrossSellCardAlternative.body.Mnme3U: Get paid while you're alive if you're diagnosed with a serious illness like cancer, heart attack, or stroke. Our Critical Illness Insurance covers 44 conditions – more than any other in Canada.
ciCrossSellCardAlternative.button.xBtJC7: Why do I need Critical Illness Insurance?
ciCrossSellCardAlternative.modalBody.VDD:
Critical Illness Insurance gives you a one-time, tax-free payment if you're diagnosed with a serious illness like cancer, heart attack, or stroke. You can use this money for anything — like paying bills, covering medical costs, or taking time off work. It helps protect your savings and gives you some financial breathing room during a tough time. {br}{br} Why does critical illness insurance cost more than life insurance for the coverage amount you get? Here's why: {br}
  • You’re more likely to use it. You’re actually 7 times more likely to get a serious illness than to pass away during your policy.
  • Some conditions offer more than one payout. If you get an early-stage illness, you could get a partial benefit without affecting your overall coverage amount, and still cover you if you’re diagnosed with a more serious condition later.
  • You're more likely to need it earlier. Critical illnesses often happen earlier in life.
*This information is based on women aged 40-49, non-smokers, from Canadian Institute of Actuaries (CIA) data.

coverageRedesign.ciWhatIsMyCoverageAmoun:
Your coverage amount is the total amount of money that you will receive tax-free if you’re diagnosed with any of the covered conditions while your coverage is active. You can use this financial support to cover anything you need while you recover. {br} {br} We recommend between three to six months of your post-tax income as a starting point. {br} {br} From there, you might want to adjust the coverage amount based on other factors, including:
  • Your budget,
  • The number of months or years of income you would want covered if ever you’re diagnosed with a major illness,
  • Any existing critical illness coverage.
Your coverage length is the fixed period of your critical illness insurance. If you’re diagnosed with any of the covered conditions during this time, we will make a lump-sum payment (your coverage amount) to you that you can use to cover anything you need.

crossSellCiFaq.death.58i7oS: What if I pass away after I claim my critical illness insurance?
crossSellCiFaq.death.aQwNsF: Your loved ones will still be able to collect your life insurance payout. That’s because your term life insurance and critical illness insurance policies are meant to cover you in different unfortunate scenarios. {br}{br} Even if you were to claim your critical illness insurance, your term life insurance policy will remain active.
crossSellCiFaq.deductible.VnSbFW: Not at all. You’ll get the full amount of your insurance payout in one lump-sum tax-free benefit after your claim has been approved.
crossSellCiFaq.difference.T2LXdX: Life insurance provides a payout to your loved ones if you pass away during your term whereas critical illness insurance provides YOU with a lump-sum payout if you’re diagnosed with any of the covered conditions. It’s there to cover any change-of-lifestyle expenses or lost income if you become ill.
crossSellCiFaq.difference.w6iiBu: How is this different from term life insurance?
crossSellCiFaq.insuranceRate.7FAr6W: Insurance rates are based on the likelihood of something happening (or not happening). Since critical illnesses are a higher probability than you passing away during your term, the rates are generally higher. {br}{br} But the good news? You need a lot less coverage than you do for life insurance because you're more likely to recover after a short time.
crossSellCiFaq.insuranceRate.fLeWQZ: Why is my critical illness insurance rate higher than my term life insurance?
crossSellCiFaq.medicalExam.LOcEEp: Nope! Since we’ve already taken a look at your medical history when you submitted your application, you’ve been approved and the rates you see here are final.

crossSellCiFaq.money.Bato04: What can I use this money for?
crossSellCiFaq.money.I7mpas: Anything you need it for. Use this financial support to cover whatever you need while you recover. Most people commonly use it to replace their income while they take a leave of absence. That means you can use it to cover any of your living expenses as well as unexpected expenses. {br}{br} You won’t need to submit receipts or a list of your expenses. Once you receive the payout, the money is yours to keep.

familyFaq.coverage.gtrM0c: Nope. Once your application is approved, you can change your coverage amount or term length.{br}{br} Just reach out via phone, email or chat to one of our non-commission-based advisors to get the right coverage, for the price that works for you.{br}{br} You can even adjust your term length after you activate your policy, as long as it’s within 5 years of when your policy was activated. familyFaq.eligibility.f7cn4z: To be eligible for PolicyMe term life insurance, you must be between the ages of 18 and 75 and be a Canadian citizen, Permanent Resident, landed immigrant or have applied for landed immigrant or Permanent Resident status.{br}{br}You must also reside in a province or territory in Canada we’re licensed in. We’re currently licensed in all provinces and territories except for Newfoundland.{br}{br}Wondering about potential requirements, like a medical exam?{br}{br}We try our best to eliminate unnecessary steps, and sometimes (like if you’re relatively healthy), a medical exam isn’t needed. In fact, 52% of applicants fall into this category.{br}{br}But if you do need an exam, don’t worry. The goal of the no-cost exam is to help us get you the best policy rate possible. It’s scheduled for your convenience at your own home and should take less than 30 minutes.
familyFaq.future.3q6Fg9: No, if you’re truthful on your application then it won’t affect your chances of getting life insurance in the future. {br}{br} Life insurance companies tap into a resource called the Medical Information Bureau (MIB) when you submit an application. {br}{br} This way, the insurer can verify your medical history to make sure it matches what you disclosed on previous applications with other insurers. {br}{br} This helps the insurer properly assess risk, keep prices down and prevent fraud. In essence, MIB is like a credit report for life insurance. {br}{br} Decisions on applications aren’t visible on MIB. We can’t see if you were approved, denied or referred to underwriting for more information. {br}{br} Worried you’ll get denied coverage because of your health history? The reality is only about 5% of people are denied coverage, usually because of a complex medical history or high-risk lifestyle (for example, a scuba diving hobby). {br}{br} If you are declined, we’ll connect you to one of our partners who offers no-medical life insurance. So you’ll still have options. {br} {br}
familyFaq.future.Rs4JuT: Will applying affect my chances of getting life insurance in the future?
familyFaq.insurance.0LRtvV: At PolicyMe, we only sell term life insurance. This is because we believe that term life insurance is an affordable product that’s best suited for most Canadian families.{br}{br}Simply put, term life insurance gives you financial security during the years when you really need it, i.e. when you have large debts like a mortgage, or people who depend on you financially.{br}{br}Using technology, we’ve streamlined the traditional insurance process resulting in a fully-underwritten life insurance policy that delivers the same-quality protection you deserve but with fewer steps and lower costs.{br}{br}
familyFaq.insurance.Em6TfR: What type of life insurance do you sell?
familyFaq.price.FNCjHk: You’ll need to apply and give us a bit more information to get your final price. If your quoted price sounds good, you can apply online. It takes most people about 20 minutes or less to complete their application. {br}{br}No phone call is needed, though you can talk to a non-commission based advisor 7 days a week if you need some advice. {br}{br}Everyone gets an instant decision. About 52% of applicants don’t need a medical exam. If a review is needed, 70% don’t need a medical followup. About 5% of cases are declined, usually because of complex medical histories or high-risk lifestyles (like a love of skydiving). {br}{br}If you’re approved, you’ll get your final price. You can then review and activate your policy.

familyFaq.step.PIRvNk: To get your quote, we need some information first about who makes up your family (that’s this page). We make it easy for couples to apply together and, if you do, you each get 10% savings for the first year. {br}{br} The next page asks for your date of birth, smoking status and gender. You can also enter your partner’s information if you want to apply together. {br}{br} After that, you’ll get an instant quote. You can adjust how much life insurance you need (we offer between $100,000 to $5M) and for how long (10 to 30 years). Your quoted price changes automatically depending on the variables you choose. {br}{br} All set? Great! Now it’s time to apply online. Most customers can complete the application in 20 minutes or less.

global.privacyPolicy.sjP4Db:
PolicyMe is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of personal information in our possession and control. This policy describes the manner in which PolicyMe collects, uses, discloses and otherwise treats personal information through our website available at policyme.com, including all subpages and successor pages (collectively referred to as the “website”) as well as through other interactions with our clients and prospective clients. {br} {br} Definitions {br} Website Visitor: any individual who accesses the PolicyMe website for any purpose {br} Prospective Client: any individual who submits an application for insurance through PolicyMe {br} Client: any individual who purchases insurance through PolicyMe {br} {br} Data Collection {br} PolicyMe needs to collect personal information from our Prospective Clients and Clients in order to provide services to you and fulfill our legal obligations. PolicyMe is committed to protecting our Website Visitors’, Prospective Clients’, and Clients’ privacy and the confidentiality of their personal information in our possession. {br} {br} All users must accept PolicyMe's Privacy Policy before submitting an application for life insurance and/or critical illness insurance. {br} If you wish to opt out of the use of your personal information by our services, we are happy to assist you. Please email us at info@policyme.com and let us know what you wish to opt-out of. Please be advised that certain uses and sharing of your information may be required to continue providing certain services to you. Please note however that under certain regulations, you may not be able to opt out of the use and sharing of your personal information, in connection with seeking, applying for, or obtaining insurance products or services from us. {br} {br} The personal information we collect is used for the following purposes:
  • helping us choose the life insurance policy that best fits you and your needs;
  • verifying your identity and other information you have provided to us;
  • providing you with insurance premium quotes;
  • providing insurers and reinsurers with the required information for applications including what is needed to underwrite your applications;
  • collecting payment for the insurance products that you have purchased through our website;
  • communicating with you regarding the insurance products that you have applied for and/or purchased through our website, as well as other products and services that may be of interest to you;
  • collecting any information and/or documents required as part of a claim process and passing them along to insurers to be processed;
  • meeting legal and regulatory requirements; and
  • as otherwise required or permitted by applicable law.
In addition, we may aggregate your Information with our other users’ Information and use that information for benchmarking and analysis purposes. You may find an overview of our data collection methods below. {br} {br} Needs Assessment:Understanding a client’s needs and wants, financial position and family information enables us to ensure that all life insurance recommendations are suitable. This is both a regulatory requirement and good business. Information collected includes: ages, health status, financial information (debts, savings, investments, monthly expenses, etc.). {br} {br} Life insurance application: The life insurance application questions we ask clients to complete elicit only the information we need for contractual requirements with the life insurance companies. Information collected includes email address, name, address, phone, birth date, identification number, driving record, medical history, lifestyle information, residency status and beneficiary information. {br} {br} Third-Party Data: PolicyMe may also use third-party services to supplement or enrich our understanding of our customers. This includes cross-referencing information like a name, email address or IP address in third-party databases, and using the information there to improve our understanding of you and your business. {br} {br} Marketing Communications: From time to time, we may obtain your consent to send you email, SMS text messages and other communications about PolicyMe products, services, or events that may be of interest to you. You can opt-out of receiving marketing and promotional email from us by following the unsubscribe instructions contained in each of our email communications. {br} {br} Customer Service/Relationship Manager: If you contact a customer service agent or Relationship Manager with a comment, question or complaint by email, text message, telephone or video chat, you may be asked for information that identifies you (such as your name, address and a phone number) along with additional information we need to help us promptly answer your question or respond to your comment or complaint. We may retain this information to assist you in the future and to improve our customer service, product and service offerings. {br} {br} Testimonials: If you provide us with a testimonial, with your consent we may post it on our Site or in other materials and media, along with your name (as needed). If you want your testimonial removed, please contact us at info@policyme.com. {br} {br} Cookies & Similar Technologies: Cookies are files with small amount of data, which may include an anonymous unique identifier. Cookies are sent to your browser from a website and stored on your computer’s hard drive. PolicyMe may use “cookies” to collect information and remarket to you. You can instruct your browser to refuse all cookies or to indicate when a cookie is being sent. We may use cookies and other tracking technology to help us remember and process your product choices, understand and save your preferences for future visits, keep track of advertisements and compile aggregate data about the Site’s traffic and interaction so that we can offer better experiences and tools in the future. We may also use these technologies to provide you with enhanced online display advertising tailored to your interests. {br} {br} Tracer Tags & Web Beacons: The website may also use a technology called “tracer tags” or “Web Beacons”. This technology allows us to understand which pages you visit on the Website. These tracer tags are used to help us optimize and tailor the Website for you and future visitors to the Website. {br} {br} Website Analytics: We may use third parties to help us gather and analyze information about the areas visited on the Website in order to better understand, evaluate and improve the user experience and the convenience of the Website. {br} {br} Safeguards {br} PolicyMe maintains reasonable administrative, technical and physical safeguards in an effort to protect personal information in our custody and control against unauthorized access, use, modification and disclosure of. {br} {br} Please understand, however, that no security system is impenetrable. We cannot guarantee the security of our databases, nor can we guarantee that the information you supply will not be intercepted while being transmitted to and from us over the Internet.‍ {br} {br} Overview of safeguards in place {br} We authorize employees and service providers to have access to clients’ personal information only on a “need to know” basis in order to fulfill their job requirements. We ensure that any third party acting on our behalf maintains reasonable and appropriate safeguards in respect of your Personal Information. Additional information about our third parties’ privacy practices is available upon request.‍ {br} {br} Safeguards for minors {br} We do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from Minors. If you are a parent or guardian and you are aware that your Minor has provided us with Personal Information, please contact us. If we become aware that we have collected Personal Information from Minors without verification of parental consent, we take steps to remove that information from our servers.‍ {br} {br} Retention policy {br} PolicyMe will retain your Personal Information while you have an active life insurance policy, and afterward if we need to do so to meet our legal obligations. {br} {br} Accuracy {br} We will make reasonable efforts to ensure that your Personal Information is accurate and complete. You may request correction to your Personal Information to ensure its accuracy and completeness. A request must be in writing and provide sufficient detail to identify your Personal Information and the correction being sought. {br} {br} Disclosure {br} When you opt to apply for a life insurance policy with one of our partner companies, we provide your account information to the respective life insurance company to fulfil the application. We also notify them of any updates or changes to your account information. We may use third-party vendors to assist us with monitoring and analyzing our website’s activity, operations and marketing assistance, hosting the website, and maintaining our database. Some of these service providers will be located outside of Canada. We may share your Information with these Third Party Vendors but only as necessary for them to perform their functions and to the extent permitted by law. Use of your Information by these Third Party Vendors is governed by their own privacy policies and procedures and not by this Privacy Policy. We and our Canadian third-party service providers may provide your personal information in response to a search warrant or other legally valid inquiry or order, or to another organization for the purposes of investigating a breach of an agreement or contravention of law or detecting, suppressing or preventing fraud, or as otherwise required or permitted by applicable Canadian or other law (which may include lawful access requests by Canadian or other foreign courts, law enforcement or governmental authorities). We may also disclose personal information where necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims and to investigate or prevent actual or suspect loss or harm to persons. {br} {br} By submitting a joint application, you give permission to PolicyMe to disclose or communicate details related to your application with your partner. You are allowing PolicyMe to communicate personal information such as the status of your application or policy, information disclosed as part of the application, results of any associated follow up requirements, your billing details, etc. It does not provide authority for your partner to make changes to your policy on your behalf such as changing your beneficiaries or cancelling the policy. {br} {br} PolicyMe makes use of an automated decision-making tool for evaluating the underwriting risk of insurance applications. Based on the Prospective Client’s application responses, their application may be automatically approved, declined, or transferred to a professional underwriter for a manual review. Anyone seeking more information about this tool is encouraged to reach out to PolicyMe by phone or email (available via our Contact Us page). {br} {br} External Sites {br} Our website may contain links to third-party websites, including, without limitation, the websites of the insurance carriers offering the third-party insurance products made available to you, but we do not endorse and we are not responsible for the content of any linked external websites or for the privacy practices of the third parties that own and operate such sites. primaryBeneficiaries.beneficiaryDistribu: How much money will each beneficiary receive if I pass?

That all depends on you! You can choose to divide your life insurance payout equally amongst all of the beneficiaries you’ve named or you can choose whether some beneficiaries should receive a higher or lower proportion than others.
primaryBeneficiaries.changeLater.fjm1Fo: Can I change my beneficiaries later?
Since your beneficiaries are revocable by default, you can change your beneficiaries at any time and as often as you’d like. {br}{br}If you choose to set any of your beneficiaries as irrevocable, you would need to get their written consent before being able to make any changes to your coverage, such as updating the coverage amount, length or the percentage of the payout that your beneficiaries would receive.
primaryBeneficiaries.childrenAsBeneficia: Can I name children as my beneficiaries?

Of course! If the children are minors under 18 years of age, we will give you the option to name a trustee. In the event of your passing, a trustee would manage the payout funds on their behalf until they become of age.
primaryBeneficiaries.heading.3uG5A9: Who would you like to name as your primary beneficiaries?
primaryBeneficiaries.passAway.a9tyxi: What happens if some or all of my primary beneficiaries have passed by the time I pass away?
primaryBeneficiaries.passAwayContent.jtt: If some of your primary beneficiaries have passed away by the time you pass away, the life insurance payout from your coverage would be divided amongst the surviving beneficiaries according to the proportions that you set.{br}{br}If all of your primary beneficiaries have passed away by the time you pass away, the life insurance payout from your coverage would be divided amongst any secondary beneficiaries that you may have named according to the proportions that you set.{br}{br}If no secondary beneficiaries have been named or if they have all passed away, the life insurance payout from your coverage would be given to your estate.
primaryBeneficiaries.whatIfInstruction.s: What if I’d like to include unique instructions for paying out my benefit to my beneficiaries?

quotesCopilotRecommendation.coreProtecti: This death benefit will give your loved ones a tax-free payout to help cover their big expenses and day-to-day living costs.
This plan balances life and critical illness coverage to protect the people and commitments that matter most to you, whether that’s replacing income, covering major expenses, or giving you flexibility if illness interrupts your life.

Since you may only need a minimum amount of life insurance right now, you can focus more on Critical Illness coverage. It pays a tax-free lump sum if you’re diagnosed with a serious illness, helping you cover bills, protect your income, and focus on recovery.

A minimum amount of coverage helps put a foundation in place today, while protecting you if your situation changes in the future. It also gives you support if you’re diagnosed with a serious illness, so you’re not relying entirely on savings during recovery.

This plan prioritizes life insurance to help cover major responsibilities like income replacement and long-term financial commitments, with critical illness coverage adding flexibility if you need time off work due to illness.

recommendation.dontNeedCoverage.JOuyqT: {hasPartner,select,true{{hasExistingCoverage,select,true{Why don't we need any additional coverage?} other{Why don't we need any coverage?}}} other{{hasExistingCoverage,select,true{Why don't I need any additional coverage?} other{Why don't I need any coverage?}}}}
In addition to looking at how much money your family would need if {hasPartner,select,true{either of you} other{you}} pass away tomorrow, we also analyzed your family’s financial picture over time to figure out how long that need exists for.

We determined that if you pass away {isTerm65,select,true{ before age {term}} other{ in any of the next {term} years}} , your family will need the additional financial support of a life insurance payout to let them maintain their current lifestyle.

But if you pass away {isTerm65,select,true{ after age {term}} other{ more than {term} years from now}} , your family will not need the additional support, because by then:

  • You will have accumulated more savings
    • {hasMortgage,select,true{
  • Your mortgage will be lower
    • } other{}}
  • Your family will have a lower number of years of future expenses

You might need coverage in the future, so it’s important to check back in with us as your family situation and finances change.

But for now, no need to spend your money on something that you don’t need.

If you still want a policy, click “see {hasPartner,select,true{our} other{my}} estimated rates” above to select your own coverage and compare quotes.
recommendation.policyExplanation1.siJNBH:
The total additional coverage needed is calculated based on all of the answers you provided in your questionnaire.

We assessed how much insurance you would {hasPartner,select,true{each need} other{need}} to maintain your current lifestyle if {hasPartner,select,true{either of you} other{you}} were to get diagnosed with one of the covered conditions and would need to replace your income while you take time off work.
recommendation.policyExplanation2.U3aIwB:
Since you’re single and don’t have any children, you don’t need life insurance. Why? Because no one is relying on your income to live.

Buying coverage before you actually need it may seem like the best way to plan for the future. But the reality is that if you don’t need the coverage today, the money you would spend on insurance premiums could be better used to contribute to retirement savings or build an emergency fund.

So save your money for now, and come back to us if your situation changes.
recommendation.policyExplanation3.dlcrpw:
If {hasPartner,select,true{either of you} other{you}} pass away tomorrow, your family would {needsAdditionalCoverage,select,true{ need} other{ not need}} the financial support of {hasExistingCoverage,select,true{an additional} other{a}} {needsAdditionalCoverage,select,true{{recmd_cov_amt_currency} life insurance} other{life insurance}} payout to let them maintain their current lifestyle.

How was this calculated? We added up the total amount of money that your family will spend if they don’t make any changes to their current lifestyle, and subtracted out all the money that will be available to your family in the future.

{needsAdditionalCoverage,select,true{{recmd_cov_amt_currency} is needed to make up the difference.} other{There is no difference to make up with insurance coverage.}}
recommendation.policyExplanation4.JevpVp: * When we project forward expenses and income, we add on inflation to make sure your family can afford to pay their bills as prices increase over time
recommendation.policyExplanation5.VdE6WT: ** The numbers you see are all “present values”, a fancy statistical technique that is used to describe how much a future sum of money is worth today

tenant.CAA.privacyPolicy:
The insurance products offered on this website are underwritten by Canadian Premier Life Insurance Company, operating as Securian Canada (“Securian Canada”). Securian Canada’s digital insurance solutions are powered by PolicyMe Corp. (“PolicyMe”). {br} {br} Securian Canada and PolicyMe (collectively “we”, “us“, our”) are committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of personal information in our possession and control. This policy describes the manner in which we collect, use, disclose and otherwise treat personal information collected through our website or websites, including all subpages and successor pages (collectively referred to as the “website”) as well as personal information obtained through other interactions with clients and prospective clients. {br} {br} Definitions {br} Website Visitor: any individual who accesses the website for any purpose {br} Prospective Client: any individual who obtains a quote or submits an application for insurance through the website or through a licensed advisor {br} Client: any individual who purchases insurance through the website or through a licensed advisor {br} {br} Data Collection {br} We collect personal information from our Prospective Clients and Clients in order to provide services and fulfill our legal obligations. We are committed to protecting the privacy of our Website Visitors’, Prospective Clients’, and Clients’ and the confidentiality of their personal information in our possession. ‍{br} {br} All users must accept the Privacy Policy before submitting an application for life insurance, critical illness insurance or health and dental insurance. ‍{br} {br} If you wish to opt out of the use of your personal information, we are happy to assist you. Please email us at service@caa.securiancanada.ca and let us know what you wish to opt-out of. Please be advised that certain uses and sharing of your information may be required to continue providing certain services to you. Please note however that under certain regulations, you may not be able to opt out of the use and sharing of your personal information, in connection with seeking, applying for, or obtaining insurance products or services. {br} {br} The personal information we collect is used for the following purposes:
  • helping us choose the life insurance policy that best fits you and your needs;
  • verifying your identity and other information you have provided;
  • providing you with insurance premium quotes;
  • providing reinsurers with the required information for applications including what is needed to underwrite your applications;
  • collecting payment for the insurance products that you have purchased through the website;
  • communicating with you regarding the insurance products that you have applied for and/or purchased through the website, as well as other products and services that may be of interest to you;
  • collecting any information and/or documents required as part of a claim process;
  • meeting legal and regulatory requirements; and
  • as otherwise required or permitted by applicable law.
{br} {br} In addition, we may aggregate your Information with our other users’ Information and use that information for benchmarking and analysis purposes. You may find an overview of our data collection methods below. {br} {br} Needs assessment: Understanding a Prospective Client’s needs and wants, financial position and family information enables us to ensure that all life insurance recommendations are suitable. This is both a regulatory requirement and good business. Information collected includes: ages, health status, financial information (debts, savings, investments, monthly expenses, etc.). {br} {br} Life insurance application: The life insurance application questions we ask Prospective Clients to complete elicit only the information needed. Information collected includes email address, name, address, phone, birth date, identification number, driving record, medical history, lifestyle information, residency status and beneficiary information. {br} {br} Third-party data: We may also use third-party services to supplement or enrich our understanding of our Prospective Clients. This includes cross-referencing information like a name, email address or IP address in third-party databases, and using the information there to improve our understanding of you and your business. {br} {br} Marketing Communications: From time to time, we may obtain your consent to send you email, SMS text messages and other communications about products, services, or events that may be of interest to you. You can opt-out of receiving marketing and promotional email from us by following the unsubscribe instructions contained in each of our email communications. {br} {br} Customer Service/Relationship Manager: If you contact a customer service agent or Relationship Manager with a comment, question or complaint by email, text message, telephone or video chat, you may be asked for information that identifies you (such as your name, address and a phone number) along with additional information we need to help us promptly answer your question or respond to your comment or complaint. We may retain this information to assist you in the future and to improve our customer service, product and service offerings. {br} {br} Cookies & Similar Technologies: Cookies are files with small amounts of data, which may include an anonymous unique identifier. Cookies are sent to your browser from a website and stored on your computer’s hard drive. We may use “cookies” to collect information about you. You can instruct your browser to refuse all cookies or to indicate when a cookie is being sent. We may use cookies and other tracking technology to help us remember and process your product choices, understand and save your preferences for future visits, keep track of advertisements and compile aggregate data about the website’s traffic and interaction so that we can offer better experiences and tools in the future. We may also use these technologies to provide you with enhanced online display advertising tailored to your interests. {br} {br} Tracer Tags & Web Beacons: The website may also use a technology called “tracer tags” or “Web Beacons”. This technology allows us to understand which pages you visit on the website. These tracer tags are used to help us optimize and tailor the website for you and future visitors to the website. {br} {br} Website Analytics: We may use third parties to help us gather and analyze information about the areas visited on the website in order to better understand, evaluate and improve the user experience and the convenience of the website. {br} {br} Safeguards {br} We maintain reasonable administrative, technical and physical safeguards in an effort to protect personal information in our custody and control against unauthorized access, use, modification and disclosure of. {br} {br} Please understand, however, that no security system is impenetrable. We cannot guarantee the security of our databases, nor can we guarantee that the information you supply will not be intercepted while being transmitted to and from us over the Internet. {br} {br} Overview of safeguards in place {br} We authorize employees and service providers to have access to Clients’ personal information only on a “need to know” basis in order to fulfill their job requirements. We ensure that any third party acting on our behalf maintains reasonable and appropriate safeguards in respect of your Personal Information. Additional information about our third parties’ privacy practices is available upon request. {br} {br} Safeguards for minors {br} We do not knowingly collect personally identifiable information from minors. If you are a parent or guardian and you are aware that a minor has provided us with Personal Information, please contact us. If we become aware that we have collected Personal Information from minors without verification of parental consent, we take steps to remove that information from our servers. {br} {br} Retention policy {br} We will retain your Personal Information while you have an active insurance policy, and afterward if we need to do so to meet our legal obligations. {br} {br} Accuracy {br} We will make reasonable efforts to ensure that your Personal Information is accurate and complete. You may request correction to your Personal Information to ensure its accuracy and completeness. A request must be in writing and provide sufficient detail to identify your Personal Information and the correction being sought. {br} {br} Disclosure {br} By submitting a joint application, you give permission to us to disclose or communicate details related to your application with your partner. You are allowing us to communicate personal information such as the status of your application or policy, information disclosed as part of the application, results of any associated follow up requirements, your billing details, etc. It does not provide authority for your partner to make changes to your policy on your behalf such as changing your beneficiaries or cancelling the policy. {br} {br} We make use of an automated decision-making tool for evaluating the underwriting risk of insurance applications. Based on the Prospective Client’s application responses, their application may be automatically approved, declined, or transferred to a professional underwriter for a manual review. Anyone seeking more information about this tool is encouraged to reach out to us by phone or email (available via our Contact Us page). {br} {br} We may use third-party vendors to assist us with monitoring and analyzing our website’s activity, operations and marketing assistance, hosting the website, and maintaining our database. Some of these service providers will be located outside of Canada. We may share your Information with these Third Party Vendors but only as necessary for them to perform their functions and to the extent permitted by law. Use of your Information by these Third Party Vendors is governed by their own privacy policies and procedures and not by this Privacy Policy. We and our Canadian third-party service providers may provide your personal information in response to a search warrant or other legally valid inquiry or order, or to another organization for the purposes of investigating a breach of an agreement or contravention of law or detecting, suppressing or preventing fraud, or as otherwise required or permitted by applicable Canadian or other law (which may include lawful access requests by Canadian or other foreign courts, law enforcement or governmental authorities). We may also disclose personal information where necessary for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims and to investigate or prevent actual or suspect loss or harm to persons. We also share certain information with CAA, the insurance plan sponsor, about coverage applied for, issued or terminated. This is for administrative and reporting purposes and to improve member experience. For residents of Quebec, this information may be disclosed outside of Quebec. {br} {br} External sites {br} The website may contain links to third-party websites, but we do not endorse and we are not responsible for the content of any linked external websites or for the privacy practices of the third parties that own and operate such sites. Before using any external website, please refer to the terms of use and privacy policies of the External Sites for more information. {br} {br} Online advertising {br} We may use third parties such as ad exchanges and data companies to serve our advertisements on the website. These companies may use cookies, tracer tags or web beacons to report certain information about your visits to the website (such as web pages you visit and your response to ads) in order to measure the effectiveness of our marketing campaigns and to deliver ads that are more relevant and tailored to you. {br} {br} To learn more about this behavioural advertising practice or to opt-out of this type of advertising, you can visit the Digital Advertising Alliance website at www.aboutads.info/choices/ or the Digital Advertising Alliance of Canada website at www.youradchoices.ca/choices. {br} {br} Questions and Concerns {br} If you have any questions or concerns about our privacy policies and practices, please send a written request to the Privacy Officer at the following email address: privacy_officer@policyme.com. {br} {br} Notification of Changes {br} We may update this privacy policy to reflect changes to our information practices. We encourage you to periodically review our Privacy Policy for the latest information on our privacy practices. {br} {br} Last updated: December 6, 2023 {br} {br} While we have always respected your privacy and safeguarded all Personal Information, we have strengthened our commitment to this goal. This is to continue to provide exceptional service to you and to comply with all laws regarding the collection, use, and disclosure of Personal Information.

whatIf.commonBeneficiariesContent.k2Nv4L: Your beneficiaries are the people who will receive your life insurance payout if you were to pass away. In 90% of cases, this financial support will be most beneficial to your spouse, if you have one.  {br}{br} If you have other people who rely on you financially or if you don’t have a partner or kids, we recommend choosing your own beneficiaries. {br}{br} In that case, you’ll be able to choose your primary and secondary beneficiaries separately.
whatIf.howToPickBeneficiaries.m7Bt8Z: How do I choose my beneficiaries?
{br}
  • Your primary beneficiaries are the first set of people who will receive the payout from your life insurance coverage if you pass away.
    • {br}
  • The payout will be shared between them according to the percentages that you set.
    • {br}
  • If you name a beneficiary who’s under the age of 18, you’ll have the option to name a trustee. This is someone who would manage the funds from the life insurance payout on behalf of your minor beneficiaries until they turn of age.
    • {br}
  • If any of your primary beneficiaries have passed away at the time a claim is submitted, the payout will be shared proportionally amongst the remaining primary beneficiaries.
    • {br}
  • If no primary beneficiaries are still living, it will be shared amongst any secondary beneficiaries you have named. Otherwise, it will be given to your estate.
    • {br}
whatIf.knowAboutPrimaryBeneficariesHeadi: What you should know about primary beneficiaries
whatIf.knowAboutSecondaryBeneficaries.O5:
• Your secondary beneficiaries are the second set of people who will receive the payout from your life insurance coverage if you pass away.
    • {br}
  • They would only receive the benefit if all of your primary beneficiaries have passed away.
    • {br}
  • The payout will be shared between them according to the percentages that you set.
    • {br}
  • If you name a beneficiary who’s under the age of 18, you’ll have the option to name a trustee. This is someone who would manage the funds from the life insurance payout on behalf of your minor beneficiaries until they turn of age.
    • {br}
  • If all of your primary and secondary beneficiaries are no longer living at the time a claim is submitted, the payout would be given to your estate.
    • {br}
whatIfSecondaryBeneficiaries.content.nyl: A secondary beneficiary is someone who would collect your life insurance payout if your primary beneficiaries have already passed away by the time you pass away. We recommend listing your children as secondary beneficiaries, if you have any. {br}{br} You do not have to add any secondary beneficiaries. If your primary beneficiaries have passed away, the money would be passed on to your estate and paid out according to your Will (or based on provincial law if you do not have a Will).
whatIfSecondaryBeneficiaries.question.T4: How do I choose my secondary beneficiaries?

whatifs.businessWhatIf.4NjuAP: When a person dies, it’s likely that any businesses he/she owns will be sold to provide money for his/her family. Sometimes, it’s required if, for example, the person has business partners and there’s a contract in place that mandates the deceased person must be “bought out.” It can also be optional if, for example, the person’s family wants the money to cover expenses. Either way, it’s important to include the value of the business (including any assets or savings owned by the corporation) in your “non-retirement savings.” {br} {br} Savings within a corporation often have large tax implications upon withdrawal so it would be wise not to assume that the full amount will be available to pay for personal or family expenses. Although the exact amount is dependent on your personal income tax rate at the time of withdrawal, for purposes of this assessment, please approximate the available amount by multiplying the savings by 80%. {br} {br} For example, if you have $100,000 of savings in an account owned by a corporation, add $80,000 to your “non-retirement savings” ($100,000 x 80% = $80,000). {br} {br} Don’t forget to add this to all other “non-retirement savings” that are in personal accounts. For example, if you have $100,000 of savings in an account owned by a corporation and $200,000 of “non-retirement savings” in a personal bank and/or investment account, then your total “non-retirement savings” would be $280,000 ($100,000 x 80% + $200,000 = $280,000).”
whatifs.buyHomeWhatIf.Dl1Vjl: First off, fingers crossed that you find your dream home! However, you should select “own” only if you currently own or are in the process of purchasing your place of residence. If you plan to buy a home in the future, but are not currently looking, please don’t select “own.” {br} {br} The time of purchase, sale price and mortgage payments you make can significantly impact your coverage needs. Until you’re certain about these details, it’s best to not include them to ensure you aren’t paying for coverage that you might not ultimately need. {br} {br} When you do buy your home, we recommend revisiting our life insurance checkup to get updated advice and understand how your coverage needs may have changed.
whatifs.debtsWhatIf.AuvZVN: One of the primary objectives of our life insurance checkup is to project how much money your family will need to cover their future expenses. One component of your family’s future expenses is the repayment of your debts.
whatifs.definedBenefitWhatIf.e2bXOl: Defined benefit pension plans are tricky as the monthly payment you’ll receive (or the death benefit your family would receive) is linked to a number of unknown factors, including: your future income, future years of service, and future economic conditions, like interest rates and stock market returns. {br} {br} However, most employers will send an annual statement indicating the estimated current value of all your future monthly payments. This will often be labelled as the “termination value” or “commuted value” of your pension statement. {br} {br} If you have this number handy, please include it in your “retirement savings.” Don’t worry if you don’t; you can proceed without including your defined benefit pension plan.
whatifs.dobWhatIf.2xdrMl: Life insurance companies use a method called “age nearest” to determine the age the insurance premiums are based upon. The age nearest method calculates the issue age based on the nearest age to your coverage date. For example, if your date of birth is February 20th, 1984 and today’s date is January 20th, 2019, the issue age would be 35, even though your actual age is 34 and you won’t be 35 for another month.
whatifs.existingCovWhatIf.F0QedY: If you already have life insurance, we will reduce the coverage amount of your new policy by the amount of coverage already in place. This will make sure that the total amount of coverage between all your policies is in line with your needs.
whatifs.expensesHighWhatIf.lyEyOa: The truth is, most people tend to underestimate their monthly expenses. When estimating monthly expenses, it’s common for people to remember large recurring items, like mortgage or rent payments, hydro bills, auto payments, cell phone bills, and childcare costs. However, it’s quite common for people to leave out the non-recurring “one-time” costs, like gifts (weddings, birthdays, anniversaries), vacations, recreational activities, tickets to concerts and sports games, taxis/Ubers and nights out with your friends or family. {br} {br} If your expenses looks off, click on the info buttons beside each category to see a description of the items included in that category. Then, feel free to adjust the numbers as you see fit to update your monthly expenses.
whatifs.genderWhatIf.utw1ac: Some health conditions are more likely to occur based on sex. As a result, sex (defined as your assigned sex at birth) is used to assess your coverage. We recognize that your sex may differ from your gender identity.
whatifs.genderWhatIf.utw1ac.sex: Some health conditions are more likely to occur based on sex. As a result, sex (defined as your assigned sex at birth) is used to assess your coverage. We recognize that your sex may differ from your gender identity.
whatifs.healthWhatIf.LkU7iz: Your health tends to be an important factor in your insurance rate. For example, if you’ve suffered from a recent heart attack, you will probably pay higher premiums than someone who hasn’t. Most people aren’t too surprised by this. {br} {br} Insurers are pretty comprehensive when it comes to assessing your health. They’ll ask you about everything from asthma to Alzheimer’s. (You can count on them being very thorough here.) Each of these illnesses has different implications for your life expectancy, and each one gets factored into your life insurance rate. {br} {br} Luckily, getting insurance with a pre-existing health condition isn’t impossible. We’re here to help you through the application process as your questions arise.
whatifs.incomeInvestmentWhatIf.BfJAQZ: Please don’t include income that you generate from investments. We will cover your investments in the “savings” section. Some examples of income generated from investments are dividends, interest payments and capital gains collected from the sale of an asset.
whatifs.incomeNonEmplWhatIf.tKEcdX: Please don’t include income that you generate from investments. We will cover your investments in the “savings” section. Some examples of income generated from investments are dividends, interest payments and capital gains collected from the sale of an asset. {br} {br} Similarly, don’t include any income that isn’t tied to your work and would still be generated if you passed away. Some examples of non-employment income are rental income on a property you own or even income you make off a business that isn’t tied to your time.
whatifs.incomeWhatIf.cRYrw2: It’s important for us to have an accurate understanding of your finances, and your income is a key aspect of your financial situation. Our calculations will use your income (along with many other factors) to evaluate how much life insurance protection your family needs. The same goes for your partner’s income. {br} {br} We never recommend more coverage than you need. This prevents you from becoming “overprotected,” keeping your costs low and freeing up money for you and your family. There are many features in our calculation that help us do this. We include your partner’s future income as money that will be available to pay for some of your family’s future expenses.
whatifs.kidsEduWhatIf.kdOGEi: If your children’s post-secondary education is something you’re planning to pay for, we’ll want to make sure that your family will have enough money to cover the costs if you were no longer around.
whatifs.kidsHousingWhatIf.7SElZM: When we project how much money will be needed to cover your children’s expenses, we’ll need to decide whether or not to include housing-related expenses such as paying off your mortgage, paying your rent and paying for utilities. If there is someone who would take your children in, we won’t include housing expenses and we’ll cover only the remaining costs like food, clothing, recreation and discretionary expenses.
whatifs.kidsWhatIf.tUqyxd: Life insurance is all about protecting the people you love. If there are people who rely on you for financial support, a life insurance will make sure they have enough money if something unexpected happens to you and your income is no longer there.
whatifs.partnerWhatIf.Gm96XI: Life insurance is all about protecting the people you love. If there are people who rely on you for financial support, a life insurance will make sure they have enough money if something unexpected happens to you and your income is no longer there.
whatifs.planningKidsWhatIf.sy0UvD: If you’re expecting, first off, congratulations! You should include your expected child (or children if you’re having twins or triplets) and specify “0” when we ask for your children’s ages. {br} {br} However, if you’re merely planning on having children in the future, don’t include them. Until you are certain about if and when you’ll be having children, it’s best to not include them in your needs assessment to ensure you are not paying for coverage that you might not ultimately need. {br} {br} When your family does grow, we recommend revisiting our life insurance checkup to get updated advice and understand how your coverage needs may have changed.
whatifs.smokeWhatIf.CD0ddJ: If you quit smoking over a year ago, we have good news for you: No matter how long or how often you were smoking, you can specify “No” for this question. Your life insurance premiums will be the same as those for a non-smoker! Wasn’t that an unexpected surprise?
whatifs.tobaccoWhatIf.FcyE2T:
You should select “Yes” if you’ve used any nicotine products in the last 12 months. Nicotine products include: {br}
  • Cigarette,
  • E-Cigarettes,
  • Pipes,
  • Chewing tobacco,
  • Vaporizers,
  • Nicotine gum,
  • Nicotine patches, or
  • 13 or more cigars.
Otherwise, select “No”. Note: Cannabis products are NOT considered to be nicotine or tobacco products.
